labels.50: Positive adjectives or adverbs ("pretty," "quite," "very," "high," "strong," "large," "cute") modifying or describing something.
labels.603: Commas separating author names or list items in formal citations and structured text.
labels.633: Numbers in the thousands, often followed by a unit or category (jobs, persons, tonnes, individuals, branches).
labels.1616: The feature activates on phrases describing differences, variations, or distinctions between categories or types of things.
labels.1626: Phrases describing organizational or institutional contexts, locations, or procedural information set off by surrounding text.
labels.2594: The feature fires on "On" appearing at the start of a new sentence or clause after a period or line break.
labels.2600: The word "heavy" appears immediately before the activation boundary in most examples.
labels.2659: Text describing physical condition, military service, or assembly requirements in instructional or biographical contexts.
labels.3121: Mentions of Great Lakes (Michigan, Erie, Ontario) or lake-related geographic locations and descriptions.
labels.3667: Phrases describing positive outcomes or benefits that follow "ensure" or similar guarantee verbs.
labels.3718: Comparative or superlative adjectives describing intensity or degree (best, darker, more).
labels.4170: The feature fires on dates in the format of month/day followed by a comma and year.
labels.5762: Contractions or expressions of first-person singular verbs following dialogue or statements.
labels.6148: Plural nouns or noun phrases followed by verbs describing actions or states of groups/entities.
labels.6176: The feature detects dollar amounts or monetary values, particularly when written with currency symbols and numeric figures.
labels.6692: The word "little" appears immediately before the highlighted text across most examples.
labels.6764: Sentence beginnings or starts of noun phrases, often capitalized words that initiate clauses or descriptions.
labels.7290: Academic journal citations and publication references in scientific text.
labels.7726: Tokens between >>> and <<< are partial words or word fragments that continue across the delimiter boundaries.
labels.7804: The feature activates on text spans containing "Assange," "Assad," "Assura," or similar proper nouns/words starting with "Ass".
labels.9249: Introductory phrases or noun phrases describing entities, organizations, or products being introduced or described.
labels.9256: Exclamatory or emphatic words and phrases that express strong emotion or emphasis, often marked by special punctuation or capitalization.
labels.9260: Text introducing or linking to media content, TV shows, movies, or entertainment articles.
labels.9271: Measurement quantities and units in cooking or recipe ingredients.
labels.9845: Transitional phrases introducing or referencing written content (articles, blog posts, essays).
labels.10813: Text containing specific numerical values, dates, or technical specifications within comparative or descriptive contexts.
labels.10829: Features capture parenthetical or appositive phrases that provide descriptive elaboration or clarification immediately following the main clause.
labels.11272: Contrasting statements where the second part negates, qualifies, or provides an alternative to the first part.
labels.11853: Phrases describing how words or expressions are used, understood, or interpreted in language.
labels.12809: Phrases about improvements to personal well-being, health, or quality of life.
labels.12830: Insertion of descriptive phrases or clauses between a noun/subject and its continuation or predicate.
labels.12932: The feature activates on phrases describing wall art, bathroom decor, or Wall Street references.
labels.13361: Phrases containing "lost" or "lose" appearing in contexts about consequences, deterioration, or emotional/philosophical loss.
labels.14367: Possessive constructions where 's marks noun ownership or attribution before a clause or descriptor.
labels.14414: Phrases indicating temporal progression or future projections about demographic or procedural changes.
labels.14856: Words ending in consonant clusters or non-English phonetic patterns in names and proper nouns.
labels.14863: Transitions between independent clauses or phrases marked by punctuation or conjunctions.
labels.14872: Comparative or contrastive phrases connecting two subjects or outcomes with "so will," "same goes for," or similar connective language.
labels.14873: Phrases describing something created, designed, or built for a specific purpose or audience.
labels.15493: Titles or headers of articles, sections, or organizations followed by descriptive content.
labels.16395: Phrases introducing new information or features added/included in something.
labels.16447: Feature detects date, timestamp, or numerical patterns with whitespace/formatting breaks.
labels.16502: Descriptive phrases or adjectives positioned between preparatory context and their modified noun or concept.
labels.16916: Seasonal references to winter holidays, Christmas markets, and cold weather periods.
labels.16962: The feature detects explanations or clarifications that follow causal or conditional statements about problems, failures, or broken things.
labels.17456: Fashion designer collections and runway show descriptions with evaluative adjectives.
labels.17944: Words ending in common suffixes or compound parts where the feature activates at morpheme boundaries (like "spackling," "resemblances," "buckling/unbuckling," "straddles," "ebullient," "cosplays," "unassailable," "knuck
labels.17961: Capitalized words or letters, often at the beginning of sentences or proper nouns.
labels.17996: The feature activates on text spanning across line breaks or section boundaries where content shifts topics or formatting changes.
labels.18017: Geographic locations or place names, particularly "Los Angeles" and similar regional/city identifiers.
